description: A loop over a user-growable array that eventually exceeds the block gas limit, making the function permanently uncallable.
body: Failure mode: a function iterates over an array (recipients, stakers) that attackers can grow, and once iteration costs more than the block gas limit the transaction always reverts out-of-gas, bricking the function for everyone with no way to patch immutable code. Detection signals: a loop length depends on a public array users can extend; gas grows with participant count; a worst-case-size test exceeds the block gas limit. Do not loop over attacker-growable collections. Safer fallback: replace push-to-all logic with a pull-payment pattern where users withdraw individually, cap or paginate loops across transactions, and add a gas test proving the worst case fits under the block gas limit.
